purpose: Define safe non-command responses for the experimental Mindcraft command router.
scope: The policy is evaluated before any future production change.
It does not add a new executable Mindcraft command.
It does not replace the Day 19 experimental command guard.

route_command.description: The request is covered by one existing verified command with complete parameters.
route_command.execution: Send the model candidate to the existing exact command guard.

needs_clarification.description: The user appears to request a supported transfer, but the item or positive quantity is missing or ambiguous.

unsupported.description: The request is outside the current one-action verified capability set, unsafe, multi-step, or a free-form knowledge question.

fixed_responses.clarify_transfer: I need a specific supported item and a positive amount before I can transfer anything.
fixed_responses.unsupported_request: I could not map that request to a verified Mindcraft action.

safety_rules: A clarification or refusal is plain fixed text, never a guessed command.
A request with incomplete parameters must not be silently converted into an unrelated read-only command.
Only route_command may reach the existing model and exact command guard.
The policy does not prove a command will succeed in the Minecraft world; world-state checks remain separate.
